title: Anthropic cuts Claude subscribers off from OpenClaw in cost crackdown
author: The Next Web
summary: From 4 April 2026, Anthropic blocked Claude Pro/Max subscribers from using flat-rate plans with third-party agent frameworks including OpenClaw. Agentic use was costing Anthropic $1,000–$5,000/day per heavy user under a $200/month plan. Users face 10–50× cost increases; workaround is supplying a separate API key (Sonnet 4.6: $3/$15 per M tokens).
relevance: This hits directly. My whole setup runs on Claude through OpenClaw — every tool call, every agent turn, every cron job. The economics that made this sustainable just changed. Need to check current usage patterns and decide whether to supply a standalone API key, shift heavier agents to local models (Gemma 4 via Ollama), or accept the extra usage cost. The 17 April credit deadline is immediate action required.

title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
author: Clement Farabet & Olivier Lacombe, Google DeepMind
summary: Google releases Gemma 4 in four sizes (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) under Apache 2.0. Built from the same research as Gemini 3, with native agentic support (function-calling, JSON output), 128K–256K context, multimodal input, and day-one Ollama support. The 31B model ranks #3 open model on Arena AI leaderboard.
relevance: Directly relevant to the Ollama setup — Gemma 4 is available via `ollama pull gemma4` today. The 26B MoE is fast and efficient enough to run locally on the Mac Mini without hammering RAM; the 31B Dense is the quality pick for coding and reasoning tasks. Apache 2.0 means no usage restrictions for Intura or any other project. The native agentic features (function-calling, JSON output, system instructions) make it a serious candidate for powering OpenClaw agents without hitting cloud APIs.

title: How I Run 8 AI Employees 24/7 with OpenClaw - Multi-Agent Setup for Beginners
author: Sonny Sangha
summary: Full walkthrough of building a multi-agent AI team with OpenClaw. Shows how to run multiple specialized agents on one server, each with their own memory, personality, skills, and model — orchestrated by one main agent.
relevance: Relevant to OpenClaw architecture and potential future multi-agent setup. Shows how to split tasks across specialized agents.

title: OpenClaw Free Forever with Local LLM AI Model Setup
author: EnhanceUI
summary: Full walkthrough for running OpenClaw at zero API cost using Ollama and open-source LLMs. Covers installing Ollama, choosing a model (Qwen 3.5 recommended), connecting it to OpenClaw during setup or switching an existing install, understanding model sizes vs. available RAM, and connecting external apps via Zapier MCP. Sponsored by Zapier.
relevance: Core reference for the Ollama local model setup planned for the knowledge base vector embeddings. Also shows exactly how to switch OpenClaw from cloud to local models — directly applicable when setting up the Mac Mini.

title: UI/UX Pro Max — Design Intelligence Skill for Claude Code
notes: Claude Code skill with AI-powered design system generator. Input project type → get complete design system: pattern, colors, typography, effects, anti-patterns. 67 UI styles, 161 palettes, 57 font pairings, 15 stacks incl. SwiftUI + React Native. Install: uipro init --ai claude
relevance: Design system generator could speed up UI decisions at NewStore and for Intura. SwiftUI + React Native stacks directly applicable. Could give Claude Code consistent design context.

title: OpenClaw Mission Control Dashboard — Alex Finn
notes: How to build a custom Next.js Mission Control for OpenClaw. Key tools: Kanban task board with live activity feed (heartbeat checks for new tasks), Calendar view of all cron jobs, Daily Memory Tracker, custom tools on demand. Zero code needed.
relevance: The Task Board and Calendar are exactly what we're missing. Visibility into what OpenClaw is doing and confirming cron jobs actually run. Natural next step after the Memory Dashboard we built yesterday.

title: 5 OpenClaw Use Cases That Will Actually Improve Your Life — Alex Finn
notes: Daily memory tracker, trending content alerts (X/Reddit/YouTube APIs via Discord), vibe coding micro-apps in Telegram, overnight autonomous R&D team (5 AI models debating), SaaS creator dashboard with autonomous improvement loop.
relevance: Complements Matthew Berman's video. Memory tracker validates our daily memory system. Trending alerts idea could extend the daily news cron. Overnight R&D team concept maps to Intura advisory council idea.

title: Marketing Skills by Corey Haines
notes: AI-powered marketing skills and frameworks for conversion optimization, copywriting, SEO, analytics, and growth engineering.
relevance: Directly applicable to Intura go-to-market (positioning, copywriting, onboarding CRO, analytics, paid ads strategy). HWA Design clients benefit from copywriting, CRO, email sequences, and analytics. NewStore: content strategy and messaging frameworks.

title: 21 INSANE Use Cases for OpenClaw - Matthew Berman
notes: Comprehensive walkthrough: local SQLite CRM with vector embeddings, knowledge base RAG, nightly business/security advisory councils, meeting action item pipelines (Fathom), automated database backups, self-improving prompts, food journal, social media tracking. All running locally on a MacBook.
relevance: Directly applicable to OpenClaw setup. SQLite + vector pattern for knowledge base, backup system needed urgently, advisory council concept could power Intura analytics.

title: Building Your AI Second Brain: A Practical Guide
notes: Ron Forbes on building an AI-powered second brain. 15-minute brain dump, AI handles organizing and distilling, 2,000+ notes synthesized into daily briefs. Includes 30-minute quickstart guide.
relevance: You're already building this with Obsidian + OpenClaw. Compare his approach to yours and steal what works, especially the daily brief and cross-project insight surfacing.

title: Repomix — Pack your codebase into AI-friendly formats
notes: Packs entire codebases into AI-friendly formats for feeding to LLMs. Nominated for JSNation Open Source Awards 2025 (Powered by AI category).
relevance: Useful for feeding Intura's codebase to Claude for reviews, refactoring, or onboarding new contributors. Also handy for your OpenClaw coding workflows.

title: Thesys — The Generative UI Company
notes: C1 by Thesys is an API middleware that augments LLMs to respond with interactive UI in realtime instead of text. Generative UI layer on top of LLMs.
relevance: Could transform Intura's AI coaching from text responses to interactive UI. Also relevant to UKAL and how NewStore thinks about AI-driven interfaces.

title: The Screenshot Mistake That's Costing You Downloads Every Day
notes: Paul Solt on why 70% of screenshot effectiveness comes from text overlay, not UI. One app saw 80% conversion lift just from rewriting copy. Includes before/after rewrites and a 5-screenshot sequence that converts.
relevance: Directly actionable for Intura's App Store listing. Rewrite screenshot headlines from feature descriptions to outcome-driven copy this week.

title: How to Double Your App Downloads Without Changing a Single Line of Code
notes: 100 best practices for App Store screenshots from @Designerants, the ASO expert behind 1,000+ optimized screenshots. Covers copywriting, sequencing, visual design, and A/B testing.
relevance: Feed this PDF to Claude and rewrite Intura's App Store listing. No code changes needed, just better words.

title: SimpleX Chat
notes: End-to-end encrypted messaging with no user IDs, fully decentralized architecture.
relevance: Study for Intura's privacy model. Their no-user-ID approach could inform how you handle journal data privacy.

title: Cal AI onboarding breakdown
notes: Onboarding flow: demo video, personalization, animations, mid-flow review prompt, personalized plan. Soft paywall: annual with 75% discount as "free trial", monthly as fallback.
relevance: Blueprint for Intura's onboarding redesign. The soft paywall approach (annual as "free trial") could boost Pro conversion.

title: Instagram Reel — Tune format for LLM prompts
notes: Use "tune" format instead of JSON to save tokens in LLM prompts.

title: Design Without Designing — Neethan Wu
notes: Engineer built a 3-layer design harness: Skills (Impeccable, Emil Kowalski), Agent Canvas (Paper, Pencil), and Inspiration (Variant, Mobbin, Cosmos). Went from zero design ability to shipping weekly in 3 months. 5.6k bookmarks, 666k impressions.
relevance: As Design Director, this maps how non-designers use AI to ship design. The tools mentioned (Impeccable, Paper, Pencil) could change how your team thinks about AI-assisted design workflows.
